content.text: Python variables and data types are how you name pieces of information and define what kind of values they can hold; mastering them (including advanced typing and the data model) lets you write robust, scalable, and Gen‑AI‑ready Python code. After working through this package, you should be able to reason precisely about how any Python value behaves, how to choose the right type, and how to communicate that to humans, tools, and LLMs.
A variable is a name that refers to a value stored in memory, created by assignment with
and binds it to the integer value
Python is dynamically typed: you do not declare types; the type is inferred from the value you assign.
(str); same name, different type at different times.
